context.story_engine: A high school teacher builds a drug empire, testing how far he'll go for family and survival

plotlines.name: Walt: Empire
plotlines.goal: build a drug business to secure his family's financial future
plotlines.obstacle: inexperience with criminal world, violent dealers like Tuco, moral boundaries

plotlines.name: Walt: Family
plotlines.goal: keep his family together while hiding his criminal activities
plotlines.obstacle: Skyler's growing suspicions, need to explain absences and behavior changes
plotlines.stakes: family breakdown, exposure of drug business

plotlines.name: Hank: Investigation
plotlines.goal: find the source of the high-purity blue methamphetamine
plotlines.obstacle: no direct leads, only chemical evidence and street-level traces
plotlines.stakes: dangerous drugs remain on the streets

plotlines.name: Walt: Treatment
plotlines.goal: undergo cancer treatment to extend his life
plotlines.obstacle: financial burden, physical side effects, family pressure

events.event: Walt works at the car wash to support his pregnant wife and disabled son
At Walt's 50th birthday party, Hank shows off his gun and invites Walt on a DEA ride-along
During the meth lab raid, Walt spots his former student Jesse escaping
Walt collapses at the car wash and is rushed to the hospital
Doctor diagnoses Walt with inoperable Stage III lung cancer, two years to live
Walt tells no one about the cancer diagnosis
Walt drives to Jesse's house and proposes a meth cooking partnership
Jesse laughs but agrees after Walt threatens to turn him in to Hank
Jesse purchases an old RV to serve as a mobile lab
Walt's chemistry produces 99.1 percent pure methamphetamine
Krazy-8 and Emilio arrive at the cook site, having followed Jesse
Emilio recognizes Walt from the DEA ride-along and accuses Jesse of working with police
Krazy-8 and Emilio force Walt and Jesse into the RV at gunpoint
Walt deliberately creates phosphine gas and runs out, jamming the door
Emilio dies from the gas, Krazy-8 collapses unconscious
Walt drives the RV away with both bodies inside, crashing into a ditch
Walt records a video confession and farewell message to his family
Walt raises a gun toward approaching vehicles that turn out to be fire trucks

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore Walt's response to mortality—building a legacy, protecting loved ones, fighting death
Walt's entry into the drug business directly creates Jesse's survival crisis
Walt hides his cancer diagnosis while simultaneously entering the drug business to provide for his family

events.event: Walt and Jesse return from the desert with the RV containing Emilio's body and unconscious Krazy-8
Jesse panics and blames Walt for the catastrophe
Walt takes charge and assigns tasks: Jesse to dispose of Emilio, Walt to deal with Krazy-8
They lock Krazy-8 in Jesse's basement using a bicycle lock around a pole
Walt goes home and lies next to Skyler, hiding all evidence of the night
Skyler notices Walt's strange behavior and presses him, but he deflects
Walt brings food and water to Krazy-8 and they have cautious conversations
Krazy-8 tells Walt about his family's furniture business and how he fell into dealing
Walt begins to feel sympathy and considers releasing Krazy-8
Jesse dissolves Emilio's body in hydrofluoric acid in a regular bathtub, ignoring Walt's instructions
The acid eats through the tub and floor, sending dissolved remains crashing through the ceiling
Walt arrives at Jesse's house and is horrified by the destruction
Walt makes Jesse clean up the gruesome mess
Walt reconstructs a broken plate from the trash and discovers a large shard is missing
Walt realizes Krazy-8 has concealed the plate shard as a weapon
Walt confronts the knowledge that Krazy-8 will kill him if freed in an agonizing internal struggle
Krazy-8 lunges at Walt with the plate shard as Walt unlocks the bicycle lock
Walt strangles Krazy-8 to death with the bicycle lock
Walt sits on the floor weeping after killing Krazy-8
Walt and Jesse bury the bodies in the desert
Skyler grows more suspicious of Walt's absences
Walt deflects Skyler's suspicions by claiming he takes long walks to process stress
Hank mentions at family dinner that the DEA is investigating new high-purity meth on the streets

interactions.description: all plotlines explore crossing moral boundaries and the consequences of desperate choices
Hank discusses investigating Walt's meth at the family dinner while Walt sits at the table
Jesse's botched body disposal directly impacts Walt's criminal operation

events.event: Walt finally tells Skyler about his lung cancer diagnosis
Skyler is devastated and immediately begins researching treatment options, oncologists, and costs
Skyler insists they fight it aggressively while Walt resists, saying treatment will bankrupt the family
Skyler schedules an appointment with an oncologist anyway and tells Marie
Hank tries to offer Walt support but Walt shuts down any emotional conversation
The family gathers for an intervention-style meeting where everyone urges Walt to pursue treatment
Walter Jr. passionately argues that his father must fight
Walt agrees to undergo chemotherapy due to family pressure
Jesse tries to sell the remaining meth to his regular contacts but discovers he has no reliable network without Krazy-8
Jesse tries selling at a biker bar and nearly gets robbed
Jesse realizes he cannot distribute the product alone and needs a new connection
Walt and Jesse meet to discuss their distribution problem and need for territory
Jesse suggests they need to establish territory by dealing with other dealers
Walt learns chemotherapy will cost thousands per session even with insurance
Walt calculates the financial burden and recognizes his salary cannot cover treatment and family security
Walt's resolve to continue cooking hardens due to financial reality
Skyler creates an online fundraising page for Walt's treatment, which humiliates him
Marie shoplifts a decorative tiara and gives it to Skyler as a baby shower gift

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore how family obligations force difficult choices—Walt accepts treatment he doesn't want, continues cooking he morally opposes, all to meet family expectations and needs
Walt and Jesse's separate struggles with the drug business merge as they realize they need each other to solve their distribution problem
the family pushes Walt toward expensive treatment not knowing he's already committed to cooking meth to pay for it

patches.reason: Marie's shoplifting behavior appears to be a separate character arc that doesn't fit existing plotlines

events.event: Walt begins chemotherapy, experiencing exhaustion and nausea
Walt hides the severity of side effects from Skyler, vomiting in private
Skyler notices Walt's deterioration and becomes increasingly protective
Walt finds Skyler's protectiveness suffocating
Walt snaps at a student at school
Walt stares at his reflection as clumps of hair fall out
Walt decides to shave his head completely
Walter Jr. shaves his own head in solidarity with Walt
Hank and Gomez investigate the new blue-tinted methamphetamine in Albuquerque
DEA traces the supply back to a low-level dealer
Hank learns the product has unusually high purity, suggesting a skilled chemist
Jesse reconnects with Badger and recruits him to sell on the street
Jesse claims territory near the crossroads
A rival dealer confronts Jesse in a standoff that Jesse wins by bluffing
Skyler discovers the baby shower tiara Marie gave her was shoplifted
Skyler confronts Marie, who denies shoplifting the tiara
The sisters' relationship fractures as Skyler refuses to speak to Marie
The conflict pulls Hank into the middle, straining both marriages
Walt and Jesse cook a second batch in the RV in the desert
Walt insists on maintaining high purity standards despite Jesse's impatience
Jesse starts selling through Badger with small amounts and thin margins
Walt calculates they need larger quantities and tells Jesse they need a real distributor

interactions.description: all plotlines explore transformation and its consequences—Walt's physical transformation from cancer treatment mirrors his moral transformation into a drug dealer, while his family relationships transform under the strain
Hank investigates the high-purity blue meth without knowing his brother-in-law is the chemist he's looking for
Walt and Jesse's business partnership requires Jesse to survive street-level dealing to build their distribution network

events.event: Skyler organizes a family gathering and invites Ted Beneke
Walt becomes uncomfortable and jealous of Ted's presence
Walt overcompensates by drinking too much
Walt forces Walter Jr. to drink tequila until he vomits
Hank confronts Walt and takes the bottle away
Skyler demands to know what is wrong with Walt beyond cancer
Walt offers no explanation to Skyler
Jesse learns about Tuco Salamanca from his distribution efforts
Jesse approaches Skinny Pete for an introduction to Tuco
Skinny Pete warns Jesse that Tuco is dangerously unpredictable
Jesse goes to Tuco's headquarters and presents Walt's blue meth sample
Tuco tests the meth and is impressed by the quality
Jesse proposes a distribution deal with Tuco
Tuco beats Jesse severely and takes the meth without paying
Jesse calls Walt from the hospital, battered and humiliated
Walt visits Jesse in the hospital and sees his injuries
Hank and Gomez process Jesse's beating as drug-related assault
Jesse refuses to cooperate with police
Walt realizes they cannot be victims in the criminal world
Walt resolves to confront Tuco himself
Walt takes the remaining meth and prepares for meeting with Tuco

episodes.theme: the cost of pride and inadequacy

interactions.description: all plotlines explore masculine inadequacy and the dangerous lengths men go to prove themselves
Jesse's beating by Tuco directly impacts Walt's business strategy and forces him into direct confrontation
Hank processes Jesse's beating unaware that it connects to Walt's criminal activities

events.event: Walt synthesizes mercury fulminate explosive disguised as meth
Walt drives alone to Tuco's headquarters
Tuco's guards let Walt inside, amused by his appearance
Walt introduces himself as Heisenberg and demands payment
Tuco laughs and threatens Walt
Walt throws mercury fulminate crystal, causing explosion
Tuco agrees to pay for stolen meth and buy future batches
Walt walks out with bag of cash
Walt returns home buzzing with adrenaline and has aggressive sex with Skyler
DEA identifies blue meth as extremely pure with advanced chemistry signature
Hank presents findings suggesting cook has advanced chemistry training
DEA investigation focuses on known drug operations but finds no match
Skyler notices Walt carrying extra cash and behaving erratically
Skyler asks pointed questions about Walt's whereabouts
Walt fabricates story about winning money at poker game
Skyler is skeptical but lets it go for now
Jesse recovers from beating and learns Walt made deal with Tuco
Jesse is unnerved by Walt's transformation into someone who confronts violent criminals
Walt and Jesse agree to cook Tuco's order immediately

interactions.description: all plotlines explore how power changes relationships—Walt's newfound dominance affects his business dealings, marriage, and partnership with Jesse
Hank correctly deduces the cook has advanced chemistry training while Walt sits at his dinner table
Walt's successful confrontation with Tuco resolves Jesse's immediate survival crisis while advancing their business partnership

events.event: Walt and Jesse cook Tuco's two-pound order in the RV, working through the night
Walt refuses to cut corners despite Jesse's urge to rush
The cook yields their purest batch yet
Walt and Jesse arrange delivery with Tuco at his headquarters
Tuco tests the product, declares it excellent, and pays fifty thousand dollars
Tuco demands double the quantity for next delivery on tighter schedule
Walt hesitates but agrees, recognizing refusing Tuco is not realistic
Skyler discovers a second cell phone hidden in Walt's belongings
Walt claims the phone was a replacement he bought at school but forgot about
Skyler does not believe Walt and begins quietly investigating on her own
Hank celebrates a promotion within the DEA's Albuquerque office
Hank describes the blue meth case at family dinner, explaining its superior quality
Walt listens intently, masking his reaction, and asks casual questions
Walt processes that the DEA is hunting his product and Hank is leading the effort
Jesse tells Walt they should find another buyer after witnessing Tuco's volatility
Walt argues that Tuco's volume and cash make him irreplaceable for now
Tuco beats his own associate nearly to death over a minor perceived slight
Walt and Jesse stand frozen, unable to intervene
Walt and Jesse understand they are trapped with a man who could kill them on impulse
Walt sits in his driveway staring at his house, family unaware of the danger

interactions.description: all plotlines explore how success comes with escalating costs—Walt's business success traps him with Tuco, threatens his family, and puts Jesse in mortal danger
the audience knows Walt is the target of Hank's investigation while the family celebrates Hank's promotion
Hank's case discussion at family dinner directly impacts Walt's awareness of the threat
